roster_context: Collapsed from 6-2 start to finish 8-9, missing playoffs for first time since 2019. Defense deteriorated badly (19th in total D, 25th in pressure rate). Massive free agent class of 17 UFAs including franchise icons Mike Evans and Lavonte David. Pass rush has been a persistent problem since Shaq Barrett's departure. New OC Zac Robinson hired after Josh Grizzard fired. Todd Bowles retained but under pressure. Offense was ravaged by injuries — only C Graham Barton played every game on the OL. Baker Mayfield returns as starter with ~$23M cap space pre-restructures.

needs.explanation: Tampa Bay's most glaring need for multiple years running. Haason Reddick had just 2.5 sacks and is unlikely to return. Chris Braswell (2024 2nd-rounder) has been invisible with only 2.5 career sacks and may be traded. David Walker (2025 4th-rounder) tore his ACL in camp and has never played an NFL game. Anthony Nelson is a career backup. YaYa Diaby is the only productive edge rusher (7 sacks in 2025) but even he isn't elite. Tampa Bay was 25th in pressure rate and had just 37 sacks in 2025. Licht and Bowles both acknowledged at the Combine that upgrading the front seven is the priority.
needs.current_starter: YaYa Diaby, Anthony Nelson (pending Reddick departure)
needs.depth: Chris Braswell (disappointing), David Walker (returning from ACL, unproven), Markees Watts

needs.explanation: Lavonte David is a pending UFA at age 36 who may retire or return on a cheap deal but can no longer be the full-time answer. SirVocea Dennis is the only off-ball LB under contract for 2026 and is undersized with coverage limitations. Bowles noted Dennis 'can be a starter if he cleans up his open-field play.' Behind those two, LB depth is almost non-existent. The Bucs need a long-term starter to eventually replace David and play alongside Dennis.
needs.current_starter: SirVocea Dennis (under contract), Lavonte David (UFA, age 36)
needs.depth: Nick Jackson (practice squad level), almost no viable depth

needs.explanation: Cade Otton is a pending UFA and could be priced out. He had 59 catches in each of the last two seasons and is a solid all-around TE but not flashy. Ko Kieft (blocking TE/ST ace) is also a UFA after suffering a broken leg in Week 3. If both depart, the only TE under contract is Payne Durham, a blocking specialist who is not a viable full-time starter. Multiple mock drafts have the Bucs targeting a TE like Kenyon Sadiq at 15.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 if Otton departs, Round 4-5 for depth if he re-signs

needs.explanation: Jamel Dean is a UFA coming off a career-best 3-INT season and is unlikely to be re-signed given the cost. Zyon McCollum (extended pre-2025) missed time late with a hip injury. The Bucs invested in 2024 draftees Benjamin Morrison (2nd round) and Jacob Parrish (3rd round), but Morrison missed 7 games with hamstring issues as a rookie and is unproven. Parrish showed flashes in nickel. Bowles said both will battle for the outside CB spot vacated by Dean. There's talent here but very little proven depth or experience.
needs.current_starter: Zyon McCollum, Benjamin Morrison or Jacob Parrish (battling for CB2)
needs.depth: Jacob Parrish (nickel), Benjamin Morrison (2nd-year), J.J. Roberts (returning from knee injury)

needs.explanation: Logan Hall (UFA) started 16 games and had 32 pressures but never blossomed into a plus starter. Greg Gaines (UFA) was a key rotational piece playing 33.5% of defensive snaps. If both leave, the Bucs are left with Vita Vea (turning 31), Calijah Kancey (played only 29 of 51 games in 3 seasons due to injuries including a torn pec in 2025), and promising but raw rookie Elijah Roberts. The DL needs more reliable bodies especially given Kancey's durability concerns.
needs.depth: Elijah Roberts (promising 5th-round rookie), Logan Hall & Greg Gaines both UFAs likely departing

needs.explanation: The ideal starting five of Wirfs-Bredeson-Barton-Mauch-Goedeke was elite in 2024 but decimated by injuries in 2025, using 9 different starting combinations. Cody Mauch tore his ACL in Week 2 and is recovering. Ben Bredeson went to IR with a knee injury in Week 14. Both are expected back but Mauch may not be 100% for Week 1. Depth behind starters is thin — Dan Feeney, Michael Jordan, and Charlie Heck are all UFAs. Graham Barton was the only lineman to play every game. Need at least one quality interior depth piece.
needs.current_starter: Ben Bredeson (LG, returning from IR), Cody Mauch (RG, recovering from ACL)
needs.depth: Elijah Klein (backup G), Dan Feeney/Michael Jordan (UFAs likely gone)

needs.explanation: Bucky Irving is the lead back under contract. Rachaad White is a UFA and likely to leave. Sean Tucker (RFA) is expected back — Licht said he wants Tucker to return. Tucker has flashed in limited work (4.3 YPC career). The Bucs could use a complementary back to pair with Irving, but this isn't a premium need given Irving's talent and Tucker's potential.
needs.depth: Sean Tucker (RFA, expected back), Owen Wright

needs.explanation: Mike Evans is a UFA at age 33 — he will return to play in 2026 but is exploring free agency and could leave Tampa. Chris Godwin is under contract but battled injuries (missed 8 games in 2025). 2025 1st-rounder Emeka Egbuka emerged as the team's leading receiver (63-938-6) and is the future. Jalen McMillan missed 14 games with a neck injury but Bowles praised his ceiling. Tez Johnson and Kam Johnson provide depth. If Evans re-signs, WR is not a major need. If he leaves, the Bucs would need to find production.
needs.current_starter: Emeka Egbuka, Chris Godwin, Mike Evans (UFA)
needs.depth: Jalen McMillan (returning from neck injury), Tez Johnson, Kam Johnson, Sterling Shepard (UFA)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 4-6 for depth, or Round 2-3 if Evans departs

free_agency_impact: Haason Reddick (UFA, 2.5 sacks) — unlikely to return, increases EDGE need to critical
Lavonte David (UFA, age 36) — may retire or return on cheap deal; either way LB needs long-term solution
Mike Evans (UFA, age 33) — will play in 2026, exploring FA; if he leaves, WR need escalates significantly
Cade Otton (UFA) — solid TE, could be priced out; if he leaves, TE becomes a top-3 need
Jamel Dean (UFA) — career-best 3 INTs in 2025; likely to depart, creates CB2 vacancy but team invested in Morrison/Parrish
Logan Hall (UFA) — started 16 games, DL depth thins considerably if he walks
Greg Gaines (UFA) — key DL rotation piece, played 33.5% of snaps
Rachaad White (UFA) — likely to leave, but Bucky Irving is established lead back
Re-signed QB Connor Bazelak (ERFA) — procedural move, backup QB secured
No franchise/transition tags used by Tampa Bay
Sean Tucker (RFA) — expected to receive tender per Licht comments
scheme_fit_notes: Bowles runs a 3-4 defense that relies heavily on blitzing and disguised pressures. EDGE rushers must be versatile OLBs who can rush and drop into coverage, not just pure speed rushers. ILBs need sideline-to-sideline range since Bowles asks them to cover RBs and TEs frequently — David's declining range in coverage was a notable problem. DL players who can line up at multiple gaps are valued (Bowles moves linemen around). CB prospects should be comfortable in man coverage given the blitz-heavy scheme. On offense, new OC Zac Robinson brings a play-action/RPO-heavy scheme that demands an effective run game and athletic TEs.
sources: buccaneers.com (official free agent profiles, depth charts, combine interviews)
pewterreport.com (Bucs beat coverage, DL analysis, secondary outlook)
yahoo sports / Bucs Wire (edge rusher and DL offseason previews)
si.com / BucsGameday (roster moves, Bazelak re-signing, Evans return news)
